MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Of Defendant Flowserve Us, Inc. As Successor-In-Interest To Durametallic Corporations
Off Calendar Oct-15-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Arturo E. Sandoval of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendant / moving party Flowserve US, Inc. as Successor-In-Interest To Durametallic Corporation
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Renato Caayon

RULING:
DEFENDANT FLOWSERVE US, INC. AS SUCCESSOR-IN-INTEREST TO DURAMETALLIC CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held. The Court announced its tentative in open court and adopted the tentative ruling after the arguments: Defendant Flowserve US, Inc. as Successor-In-Interest To Durametallic Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Specially, defendant's separate statement fails to lay out undisputed material facts demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that the Durametallic packing and/or gaskets to which he was exposed contained asbestos. Plaintiff's objections to Robert Smith's declarations (defendant's Exhibits E and F) are overruled except as to paragraph 6 of Exhibit E, which is sustained. However his declarations still fail to sustain defendant's initial burden of negating an essential element of plaintiff's claim, i.e., the asbestos content of the Durametallic packing and/or gaskets. Counsel for plaintiff is to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Tara L. Williams of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13428 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
